npcs.name: Vel Morrow
npcs.description: Covenant deserter. Combat trainer. She watches the slopes with the eyes of someone who knows she's being hunted.

dialogue.greeting: You made it out of the cave. That means you can fight. But can you fight *well*?
dialogue.respec_prompt: I've seen your technique. It's wrong — not fatally, but wrong enough to get you killed up here. I can retrain you. It'll cost gold and it'll hurt.
dialogue.respec_confirm: Hold still. This part you won't enjoy.
dialogue.respec_complete: Better. Not good — better. Don't make me do this twice.
idle: The wind up here covers footsteps. Remember that.
I trained with the Covenant's best. Then I killed two of them. Don't ask which two.
The peaks test everything the cave didn't. Strength isn't enough anymore.
Someone's been following me since the treeline. If they show themselves... don't interfere.

npcs.name: Edric Thorne
npcs.description: Heretic acolyte. Healer. His faith is cracking but his belief in people isn't.

dialogue.greeting: The Covenant says the gods will return. I'm starting to think they won't. But *we're* still here.
dialogue.guild_prompt: I've been thinking. Down in the cave, we survived alone — barely. Up here, alone gets you killed. What if we organized? Not a church. Not a hierarchy. Just... people who choose each other.
dialogue.guild_create: A guild. Yes. Give it a name — something worth fighting for. I'll handle the rites. Old habit.
dialogue.guild_created: It's done. Not blessed by any god, but witnessed by a man who used to speak for one. That has to count for something.
idle: I still pray. I just don't know who's listening anymore.
The Covenant would call this heresy — organizing outside the church. Good.
Tal says I worry too much. Tal also nearly died buying supplies last week. We need structure.
Every guild that forms up here is proof the gods aren't necessary. That terrifies me. And gives me hope.
